overlays: generic token-budget advice + coder-agent working-file rule
extraction_notes: Operations distilled from Aider's /add /read /drop /tokens command surface plus the ~25k distraction threshold, generalized to any coder harness with an editable working set. The defining move of this skill is the read-vs-edit distinction: breadth (finding files) is delegated to repo-map's read-only signature map; the write-set is reserved for files actually being edited this turn. Operation names are conventions; behaviors are framework-agnostic.

core_thresholds.threshold_nature: signal/dilution (accuracy starts dropping past it), not a hard ceiling; lower for weaker models

candidates_considered.name: classify
candidates_considered.rationale: The defining first step: ask 'will I edit this file's bytes this turn?' to sort into EDIT / READ-ONLY / NAVIGATE-ONLY. Without this the whole read-vs-edit distinction collapses.

candidates_considered.name: locate-then-add
candidates_considered.rationale: Backed by the 70.3% repo-map figure: locating files needs only the read-only map, never the write-set. Enforces find-then-edit two-step. Shared philosophy with repo-map skill.

candidates_considered.name: add
candidates_considered.rationale: Adding to the editable working set, deliberately restrained. Maps to Aider /add, Cursor @file, Claude Code Read-then-Edit.

candidates_considered.name: read
candidates_considered.rationale: Read-only reference layer (schema/config/CONVENTIONS). Distinct write permission from add. Aider /read.

candidates_considered.name: budget-watch
candidates_considered.rationale: Monitoring the ~25k budget is first-class given the dilution threshold. Maps to /tokens. Without monitoring the discipline is unfalsifiable.

candidates_considered.name: drop
candidates_considered.rationale: Reclaim budget on the file axis once a file's edits are done. Treats the working set as a sliding window, not an accumulator. The 'brave /drop' operation.

candidates_considered.name: lean-on-map
candidates_considered.rationale: Explicitly moves 'understand-only' files out of the write-set back to the read-only map. The antidote to the 'add to feel safe' instinct. Cross-links to repo-map.

candidates_considered.name: split-task
candidates_considered.rationale: When map-shrink and drop can't recover budget, the task is too wide. Split into multi-session subgoals. Maps to Aider token-limit guidance / subtree-only.

candidates_considered.name: clear-history
candidates_considered.rationale: Reclaims budget on the conversation-history axis, not the file axis. Belongs to session-state-hygiene; referenced as the complementary partner sharing the same 25k budget, but not owned here to keep the file-vs-history seam clean.

candidates_considered.name: switch-larger-model
candidates_considered.rationale: Explicitly flagged as an anti-pattern in SKILL.md: a larger context window does not change the ~25k dilution threshold. Excluded from the operation set on purpose.

candidates_considered.name: build-repo-map
candidates_considered.rationale: Building/refreshing the signature map is repo-map skill's territory. This skill only consumes the map (via lean-on-map / locate-then-add).

dilemmas_extracted.summary: Need to understand 10 files but only edit 2: add all (dilution) vs split into 2 write-set + 1-2 read + 6-7 navigate-via-map. Resolved by the read-vs-edit classification and the 70.3% figure.

Mid-task budget breaches 25k: cost-ordered tree — inspect /tokens, drop done files, shrink map, then (history axis) /clear via session-state-hygiene, finally split-task. File axis before history axis before task split.
Model keeps editing wrong files: counter-intuitively NOT fixed by adding more files. Either the target isn't in the working set (add it) or there are too many unrelated files (drop them).

anti_patterns: dump entire repo into editable context
use the write-set for breadth/understanding instead of repo-map
switch to a larger-window model before trimming the working set

cross_framework_interfaces.claude_code: Read tool (no read/write split; read==editable) — discipline is self-imposed via Grep/Glob for breadth, Read only for edit targets
cross_framework_interfaces.cursor: @file / @folder mentions — discipline is 'mention what you edit, not whole folders'
cross_framework_interfaces.cline: on-demand read_file tool-calls — working set is implicitly the set of files read; reading == budget cost
